description: prove cwp <-> cosign/sigstore DSSE interop at the Ed25519ph layer, both directions (P0-T03)
rules: cosign/sigstore sign DSSE with Ed25519ph (HashEdDSA over SHA-512 of the PAE), not pure Ed25519
cwp native signatures stay pure Ed25519; the ph gap is bridged via OpenSSL (infra/cwp/cosign.py)
direction A: cwp.verify_ph accepts a REAL cosign envelope (fixture) and rejects tamper
direction B: cwp.sign_ph output verifies under the Go anchor's ed25519ph mode — cosign's own engine
usage: Set COSIGN_ENVELOPE (a real cosign attest-blob DSSE envelope) + COSIGN_PUB (its Ed25519 public-key PEM). Output: interop.json. Exit 0 iff cwp verifies cosign's output (and rejects tamper) AND cwp's ph output verifies under the Go ed25519ph anchor.
limitation: cosign's CLI verify-blob-attestation --key cannot verify a key-based ed25519ph attestation (it fails on cosign's OWN output — an upstream cosign limitation), so direction B uses the Go anchor (crypto/ed25519.VerifyWithOptions{SHA512}), which IS cosign's verification primitive, rather than the cosign CLI. Requires openssl>=3.4 (for ed25519ph) + the go toolchain.
